title: erigeron koraginensis
intro: Erigeron koraginensis, also known as the "Russian fleabane" or "Kodiak daisy," is a perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to the coastal regions of Alaska and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Erigeron koraginensis has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and digestive problems, and is also said to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits.
